title: Algebras of order parameters in one-dimensional spin systems
authors: Ameya Chavda, Clement Delcamp, Alex Turzillo, Minyoung You
abstract: We study order parameters in one-dimensional quantum lattice models with finite invertible or non-invertible symmetry. We investigate what properties a string operator must satisfy in order to acquire a non-vanishing expectation value in a given gapped phase. We deduce that multiplets of string order parameters organise into a Lagrangian algebra in the Drinfel'd centre of the symmetry category. In particular, we highlight the role of the multiplication rule as governing the fusion of the twisted sector local operators that constitute the string operator in the infrared limit. Our derivations exploit the tensor network approach to the classification of gapped phases and its reformulation in terms of module categories over the symmetry category. Within this framework, a gapped phase is associated with a pattern of spontaneous symmetry breaking wherein a Morita class of algebras of topological lines is preserved in the ground state subspace. The crux of the proof is to show that the expectation value of any string operator explicitly depends on the tube algebra module associated with the Lagrangian algebra, which is realised as the full centre of the corresponding module category. Finally, we demonstrate that these techniques extend to phases of symmetric mixed states.
summary: This paper provides a unified algebraic framework to classify string order parameters in 1D systems with non-invertible symmetries. It demonstrates that these order parameters form a Lagrangian algebra in the Drinfeld center of the symmetry category. The results extend the understanding of phase transitions and symmetry breaking to both pure and mixed-state quantum systems.
main_problem: The paper investigates the algebraic properties of string order parameters in 1D quantum lattice models with both invertible and non-invertible symmetries, specifically focusing on the role of the multiplication rule in the Lagrangian algebra.
main_result: The authors prove that multiplets of string order parameters organize into a Lagrangian algebra within the Drinfeld center of the symmetry category and that the expectation value of a string operator depends on the associated tube algebra module.
method: The study utilizes tensor network approaches, specifically block-injective Matrix Product States (MPS), combined with the mathematical framework of fusion category theory, module categories, and the SymTFT construction.
model_or_system: One-dimensional quantum lattice models (spin systems) characterized by finite invertible or non-invertible (fusion) symmetries, including both gapped phases and symmetric mixed states.
key_observables: String order parameters, twisted sector local operators, and expectation values of non-local operators.
important_parameters: Gapped phases, spontaneous symmetry breaking (SSB) regimes, infrared (IR) limit, and non-anomalous symmetry categories.
main_assumptions: The symmetries are assumed to be finite and semi-simple (fusion categories), and the gapped phases are represented by block-injective MPS.
paper_structure: The paper moves from the classical Landau paradigm to SPT phases and non-invertible symmetries, establishing a unified algebraic description through tensor networks, classifying gapped phases via module categories, and providing explicit character and Omega-symbol derivations for the Drinfeld center.
why_interesting: This work is highly relevant for many-body dynamics and open quantum systems as it extends the classification of order parameters to symmetric mixed states and provides a rigorous algebraic framework for non-invertible symmetries.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
